config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar hanya demi kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, mayoritas bisa menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat kebijakan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat pribadi. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati dan kepentingan bersama dijaga sekaligus.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily "the common good" can become a cover for abuse of minorities. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation: public safety, disease control, and basic laws do limit what people can do. The key difference is that those limits should be narrow, justified, and subject to real checks, not a blank check to override personal liberty. In my view, a healthy society protects the public good by respecting individual rights first, then limiting them only when there is a clear and necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan itu berbahaya karena bisa menutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa terhadap kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya suara. Saya setuju ada keadaan tertentu seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan publik yang memang perlu pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan jadi alasan untuk menginjak hak dasar. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa merendahkan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, history makes me wary of any broad claim that the common good justifies overriding personal liberty, because that language has often been used to excuse discrimination, censorship, and abuse of minorities. I do think there are narrow exceptions for things like public health, emergency response, and criminal law, but those exceptions should be tightly limited and heavily scrutinized. To me, a society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default and only restricts them when there is a clear, necessary, and accountable reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya berlebihan. Pengalaman kita menunjukkan bahwa kalau hak individu tidak dijadikan batas yang tegas, kelompok kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang lemah mudah dikorbankan atas nama ketertiban. Saya tidak menolak pembatasan sama sekali, tetapi pembatasan harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi, misalnya untuk wabah atau bencana. Jadi, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From a United States perspective, I am especially skeptical of that idea because "for the public good" has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and other abuses against people with less power. I do think there are real exceptions, like public health emergencies, criminal law, and disaster response, but those limits have to be narrow, necessary, and accountable. To me, the burden should be on society to justify any restriction on rights, not on the individual to prove why their rights should matter.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
